items.title: The Fluid Mosaic Model of chromatin organization: integrating dynamics, conformational heterogeneity and multiscale organization
items.author: Ajazul H. Wani
items.content: The Fluid Mosaic Model, previously used to describe membrane structure and dynamics, is proposed here to explain the complex structural organization and dynamics of eukaryotic chromatin.

items.title: Ferritin light-chain contributes to hair cells function and survival
items.author: Régis Nouvian
items.content: Deletion of the light-chain of ferritin sheds light on the role of iron homeostasis in survival and function of hair cells of the cochlea in a mouse model.

items.title: In vivo activities of E1 elements in the insulin promoter
items.author: Issei Saitoh
items.content: The E1 elements in both Ins1/2 promoters are regulated for the in vivo transcription of insulin genes in mice.

items.title: Data-driven mouse motor thalamus model reveals topography and spatial weight scaling govern spindle dynamics
items.author: Alessandra Pedrocchi
items.content: Data-driven 3D modeling of mouse motor thalamic nuclei reveals that topography and spatial weight scaling are necessary for physiological spindle oscillations, uncovering architectural design principles of spatially organized thalamic circuits.

items.title: Integrative structural analysis of the human LRP2–LRPAP1 complex reveals multiple regulatory sites
items.author: Francesca Coscia
items.content: Structural studies of the human LRP2-LRPAP1 complex reveal that multiple LRPAP1 molecules can bind to the receptor, highlighting new binding sites that may control how LRP2 binding to other ligands is regulated in health and disease

items.title: Wolbachia-driven host miRNAs mediate arthropod reproduction in a Wolbachia density-dependent manner
items.author: Xiao-Yue Hong
items.content: Wolbachia-driven host miRNAs influence reproduction in the spider mite Tetranychus truncatus, with effects associated with Wolbachia density.

items.title: Nrf1 coordinates proteasome activity and autophagy to maintain cardiac proteostasis
items.author: Miao Cui
items.content: The transcription factor NRF1 coordinates dual proteasome- and autophagy-mediated protein quality control pathways to protect the heart against ischemic injury.

items.title: Multiscale adaptations underlie bite force variation in rodents
items.author: Nicolai Konow
items.content: Multi-scale analysis of the rodent craniofacial system reveals correlated bite force adaptations at the molecular, muscular and skeletal scales, that further correlate with feeding ecology.
